summary: The City of Light needs little introduction. Paris enchants with its world-class museums, iconic landmarks, unparalleled cuisine, and effortlessly chic neighbourhoods. Beyond the Eiffel Tower lies a city of hidden courtyards, vibrant markets, and neighbourhood bistros that reward the curious traveller.
neighbourhoods: Le Marais (Jewish quarter, gay-friendly, trendy boutiques), Saint-Germain-des-Prés (literary cafes, galleries), Montmartre (Sacré-Cœur, artists, bohemian), 7th arrondissement (Eiffel Tower, Musée d'Orsay), Latin Quarter (students, Shakespeare and Co), Canal Saint-Martin (hipster bars, bobo), Belleville (multicultural, street art)
must_do: Louvre Museum (book skip-the-line), Eiffel Tower at dusk, Musée d'Orsay, Sainte-Chapelle stained glass, Versailles day trip, Père Lachaise cemetery, Marché d'Aligre, Seine river cruise, café au lait and croissant at a zinc bar
transport_tips: Paris Métro is extensive and efficient. Get a Navigo Semaine pass (~$30 AUD) for unlimited travel Mon-Sun. Vélib' bike-share great for short trips. Taxis and Uber available but expensive. Eurostar from London St Pancras (2h15m). Charles de Gaulle airport: RER B to central Paris (~$20 AUD, 35 min).
safety_notes: Pickpockets active around Eiffel Tower, Sacré-Cœur, Métro line 1. Keep bags close. Avoid unofficial taxi touts at airports. Some areas near Gare du Nord can be rough at night. Scam artists common at tourist hotspots.
budget_notes: Budget: $150-180 AUD/day. Mid-range: $250-350 AUD/day. Luxury: $500+ AUD/day. Set lunch menu (formule) $15-25 AUD. Brasserie dinner $40-60 AUD. Accommodation from $80 (budget hotel) to $500+ (boutique hotel). Museum Pass 4-day ~$80 AUD saves money.

sample_day_blocks.activity: Walk Tuileries Garden, cross Seine to Musée d'Orsay
Dinner in Le Marais, wine at a cave à vins
